description_long: This object appears to be a highly damaged or fragmented airplane, with only a portion of the fuselage and wing structure remaining. The surface is mostly a dull gray, with visible panel lines and a few colored markings, including a prominent red area and a red cylindrical object that may be a fire hydrant or part of the wreckage. The wings are irregularly shaped and jagged, suggesting severe structural compromise, and there are no visible engines, tail, or intact landing gear. The object is extremely thin in profile, with little to no vertical structure remaining. The overall appearance is that of a wrecked or deconstructed airplane, possibly part of an urban crash site or a derelict display.
description: A severely damaged, fragmented airplane with jagged wings, gray surface, and red markings, lacking engines and tail.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the jagged, irregular wing edges, gray surface, and a red cylindrical object near the center.
description_view_1: Side profile revealing the extreme thinness of the object and the protruding red element.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view highlighting the fragmented structure, visible panel lines, and the red marking on the surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the thinness and the red cylindrical feature.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight, highly fragmented with jagged edges and no visible dihedral or winglets.
attribute_tail_design: No tail structure visible; tail section appears to be missing or destroyed.
attribute_landing_gear_type: No landing gear visible; possible remnants only.
attribute_cockpit_placement: No cockpit structure visible; likely destroyed or missing from the remaining fuselage.
